entries.name: AI Arsenal Digest — 2026-07
entries.description: July 2026 digest: 15 new benchmark entries across all 7 categories, a formal Trending vertical (5 commits), and a rounded-out Skills curriculum with 4 learning paths and 8 guides.

entries.name: Add A Max Step Budget To Every Agent Loop

Add Hybrid Search for Exact-Match Terms

entries.name: GQA: Training Generalized Multi-Query Transformer Models from Multi-Head Checkpoints
entries.description: Introduced grouped-query attention — sharing each key/value head across a group of query heads — cutting KV-cache memory several-fold with near-zero quality loss; now the default attention configuration in almost every open LLM

entries.name: FlashAttention: Fast and Memory-Efficient Exact Attention with IO-Awareness
entries.description: Reframed attention as an IO problem: tiling and online softmax keep the computation in GPU SRAM, avoiding materializing the N×N matrix — exact attention, 2-4x faster and linear memory, now compiled into effectively all training and serving stacks

entries.name: DeepSeek-R1: Incentivizing Reasoning Capability in LLMs via Reinforcement Learning
entries.description: Showed RL with automated, verifiable rewards (not human labels) can train strong reasoning directly, then distills into smaller dense models -- consider RL-from-verifiable-rewards for reasoning-heavy domains, not just human-feedback alignment

entries.name: DeepSeek-V3 / R1
entries.description: DeepSeek open-weight MoE and reasoning model family known for strong cost-performance

QLoRA: Efficient Finetuning of Quantized LLMs
entries.description: Showed you can fine-tune a 4-bit-quantized frozen base model with LoRA adapters trained in full precision, meaning you should use QLoRA when you need to fine-tune a large model on a single consumer GPU that couldn't otherwise fit it

entries.name: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding
entries.description: Showed bidirectional masked-language-model pretraining beats left-to-right pretraining, meaning you should reach for an encoder-only model (not a decoder-only LLM) for classification/embedding tasks

entries.description: Built a knowledge-graph index with hierarchical community summaries for global, holistic corpus queries -- reach for GraphRAG only for 'summarize the whole dataset' queries, given its indexing cost runs 100x-6000x that of standard vector RAG

entries.description: Practical guide to embeddings for retrieval, clustering, semantic search, and RAG

entries.description: Writes practical essays on retrieval, recommendation, evaluation, and ML systems

entries.name: GPTQ: Accurate Post-Training Quantization for Generative Pre-trained Transformers
entries.description: Showed one-shot post-training quantization to 3-4 bits is feasible for 100B+ param models in hours with minimal accuracy loss -- reach for GPTQ (or AWQ) as a default quantization option before more disruptive approaches

entries.name: Prompt Engineering Fundamentals
entries.description: Practical prompt engineering fundamentals for production LLM applications

entries.name: Precise Zero-Shot Dense Retrieval without Relevance Labels
entries.description: Showed you can retrieve well in a zero-shot setting by generating a hypothetical answer first and embedding that instead of the raw query, meaning you should reach for HyDE specifically when you have no labeled relevance data to train or fine-tune a retriever

entries.name: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Large Language Models: A Survey
entries.description: The standard map of the RAG landscape: organizes the field into Naive, Advanced, and Modular RAG paradigms and systematizes retrieval, generation, and augmentation techniques — the orientation document for anyone building retrieval systems

entries.description: NVIDIA's open-source LLM vulnerability scanner: automated probes for jailbreaks, leakage, injection, and toxicity

entries.description: Introduced selective state-space models with input-dependent dynamics and a hardware-aware parallel scan, achieving Transformer-quality language modeling with linear-time inference — the strongest attention alternative, now shipping in hybrid production models

entries.description: Microsoft guidance library for controlling and constraining language model generation

entries.description: Focuses on practical LLM evaluation, error analysis, and product-quality workflows

entries.description: Builder behind LangChain and LangGraph ecosystem for LLM apps and agents

entries.description: Proxy-based LLM observability platform for logs, costs, caching, experiments, and analytics

entries.name: Measuring Massive Multitask Language Understanding
entries.description: Introduced MMLU: 57-subject multiple-choice knowledge exam that became the field's default capability number for half a decade — now saturated and largely superseded (MMLU-Pro, GPQA), but still the single most-cited benchmark score in model cards

entries.name: Hugging Face Inference Endpoints
entries.description: Managed Hugging Face service for deploying models as production inference endpoints

entries.name: Training Compute-Optimal Large Language Models
entries.description: The Chinchilla paper: showed contemporary LLMs were dramatically undertrained — compute-optimal training scales parameters and data equally (~20 tokens per parameter), and a 70B model trained on 1.4T tokens beat a 280B model — resetting how the field allocates training compute

entries.name: Hold Out an Eval Set Before Any Training Run Touches the Data
Honen
entries.description: Transform any content into interactive AI-generated courses

entries.name: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models
entries.description: Showed you can fine-tune a large model by training only small low-rank update matrices injected into attention weights, meaning you should use LoRA (not full fine-tuning) whenever GPU memory or storage for many fine-tuned variants is a constraint

entries.name: A Survey on Hallucination in Large Language Models: Principles, Taxonomy, Challenges, and Open Questions
entries.description: The reference taxonomy for hallucination: splits it into factuality vs faithfulness, traces causes through data, training, and inference, and catalogs detection benchmarks and mitigation strategies — the structured map behind grounding and guardrail engineering

entries.name: Hugging Face Hub
entries.description: Model, dataset, and Space hosting platform for sharing and versioning AI artifacts

entries.name: Humanloop
entries.description: A platform for prompt management, evaluation, and product feedback workflows

entries.description: AI image generation platform with reliable text rendering and broad style coverage

entries.description: Conceptual guide to LLM serving performance: batching, KV cache, speculative decoding, and latency metrics

entries.description: A library for extracting typed structured outputs from language models

Document Q&A Pipeline
entries.description: Parse, chunk, and index documents into Postgres via pgvector, with provenance metadata and a citation-backed Q&A query

entries.name: Multi-Tool Agent
entries.description: A bounded multi-tool agent with validated arguments, Pydantic-typed final answers via Instructor, and Langfuse tracing

entries.name: Production RAG API
entries.description: A FastAPI RAG service backed by Qdrant with request-scoped tracing, source citations, and async ingestion

entries.name: Jan
entries.description: Open-source, offline-first ChatGPT alternative desktop app powered by llama.cpp

entries.name: Jason Liu
entries.description: Works on structured extraction, Instructor, evals, and practical LLM app patterns

entries.name: Jay Alammar
entries.description: Creates visual explanations of transformers, embeddings, and language models

entries.name: Jeremy Howard
entries.description: Teaches practical deep learning and makes AI education accessible to builders

entries.name: Jerry Liu
entries.description: Builder focused on data frameworks, RAG, document agents, and retrieval workflows

entries.name: Memory is Reconstructed, Not Retrieved: Graph Memory for LLM Agents
entries.description: Replaces static retrieve-then-reason agent memory with active reconstruction over a Cue-Tag-Content graph -- the agent iteratively explores and prunes retrieval paths as evidence accumulates, gaining up to 23% on LoCoMo/LongMemEval while cutting tokens

entries.name: Mixtral of Experts
entries.description: Proved sparse mixture-of-experts works at open-weights scale: Mixtral 8x7B matched or beat Llama 2 70B while activating only 13B parameters per token, making MoE the default architecture for efficient frontier models

entries.name: Jina AI Reader
entries.description: Reader endpoint for converting web pages into LLM-friendly text and Markdown

entries.name: Dense Passage Retrieval for Open-Domain Question Answering
entries.description: Showed a simple dual-encoder trained with in-batch negatives beats BM25 for passage retrieval — the paper that made dense embedding retrieval the default, and the direct ancestor of every embedding model powering today's RAG stacks

entries.name: Keep a Kill Switch for Risky Agent Tools
Keep Local Model Weight Files Out of Git
Store Source Page and Section Metadata With Every Chunk
ColBERT: Efficient and Effective Passage Search via Contextualized Late Interaction over BERT
entries.description: Introduced late interaction: keep one vector per token and score via MaxSim at query time, capturing term-level matching that single-vector retrieval loses — the architecture behind ColBERTv2/PLAID and modern multi-vector rerankers

entries.name: Khoj
entries.description: Self-hostable AI second brain: chat over your notes and documents, custom agents, scheduled automations, and deep research across local or hosted LLMs

entries.name: Kimi K2
entries.description: Moonshot AI's trillion-parameter open-weights MoE model optimized for agentic tool use and coding, with 32B active parameters

entries.name: Kimi K2.5
entries.description: AI assistant with deep understanding, analysis, and reasoning capabilities

entries.name: KServe
entries.description: Kubernetes-native model-inference platform (CNCF) with serverless autoscaling and standardized inference protocol

entries.name: Efficient Memory Management for Large Language Model Serving with PagedAttention
entries.description: Applied OS virtual-memory paging to the KV cache: non-contiguous fixed-size blocks eliminate the 60-80% memory waste of contiguous allocation, enabling 2-4x serving throughput — this is the vLLM paper, and paged KV caches are now universal

entries.name: Label Studio
entries.description: An open-source data labeling platform for ML and AI datasets

entries.name: LanceDB
entries.description: Developer-friendly embedded and serverless vector database for multimodal AI retrieval

entries.name: LangChain
entries.description: A framework for composing LLM applications, retrieval flows, tools, and agents

entries.name: Fast Inference from Transformers via Speculative Decoding
entries.description: Showed a small draft model's guesses can be verified in parallel by the full model with zero change to the output distribution, meaning production inference engines should implement speculative decoding to cut generation latency without sacrificing exactness

entries.name: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
entries.description: Coined 'RAG': a jointly-trained retriever plus generator beats closed-book generation on knowledge-intensive tasks -- ground generation in retrieved documents, though production RAG today uses a simpler decoupled pipeline

entries.name: The Mirage of Optimizing Training Policies: Monotonic Inference Policies as the Real Objective for LLM Reinforcement Learning
entries.description: LLM RL optimizes the training-engine policy, but you deploy the inference-engine policy -- and the two disagree on trajectory probabilities even with synced weights. Proposes MIPI/MIPU to make the deployed policy the optimization objective

entries.name: Lilian Weng
entries.description: Publishes deeply researched explainers on agents, alignment, diffusion, and LLM techniques

entries.name: AWQ: Activation-aware Weight Quantization for LLM Compression and Acceleration
entries.description: Showed ~1% of weight channels are 'salient' — identified by activation magnitudes, not weight values — and protecting them via per-channel scaling enables accurate 4-bit weight quantization without backpropagation; AWQ is now a standard deployment format

entries.description: Efficient fine-tuning toolkit for Llama, Qwen, Mistral, and other open models

entries.name: Unstructured
entries.description: Open-source document ETL for converting complex files into structured data for LLM pipelines

entries.name: UpTrain
entries.description: Open-source LLM evaluation toolkit with 20+ prebuilt checks for RAG quality, safety, and conversation metrics

entries.name: Allocate a Fixed Token Budget to Each Prompt Section
Wrap Retrieved Context in Explicit Delimiters
Add the Failing Question to Your Eval Set Before Fixing the Bug
Prefer INT8 Over INT4 When Quality Risk Matters More Than Maximum Compression
Request JSON Only When a Parser Will Consume the Output
Constrain Critical Outputs With a JSON Schema, Not Prose Instructions
Default to Local Models When Prototyping With Sensitive Data
Apply Metadata Filters Before Similarity Search
Use Parent-Child Chunking to Balance Precision and Context
Rewrite Vague Queries Before Embedding Them
Use Rubric-Anchored Prompts for LLM Judges, Not Bare Score Requests
Cache Answers for Semantically Similar Repeated Questions
Return Sentence Windows for Dense Manuals and Policy Docs
Use Small Models or Rules for Routing, Classification, and Extraction Sub-Steps
Use Speculative Decoding When a Cheap Draft Model Is Available and Latency Dominates
Wrap Long Prompt Sections in XML Tags for Clear Parsing
Vaani
entries.description: Fast, private macOS dictation with AI formatting and editing

entries.name: Validate LLM Judges Against Human Labels Before Trusting Their Scores
Validate Tool Arguments Before Execution, Not Inside the Tool
Attention Is All You Need
entries.description: Showed self-attention alone (no recurrence or convolution) can model sequences, enabling fully parallelised training that made today's LLM-scale training runs computationally feasible

entries.name: Vector Search Tools
entries.description: Curated tools for vector search workflows in AI engineering

entries.name: Vercel
entries.description: Frontend cloud platform for deploying and scaling Next.js apps with edge functions

entries.name: Vercel AI SDK
entries.description: The standard TypeScript toolkit for AI apps: one provider-agnostic API for text, structured output, tool calling, and agents with React/Next.js streaming UI

entries.name: verl
entries.description: ByteDance's flexible RL training library for LLMs implementing the HybridFlow paper, powering large-scale GRPO/PPO reasoning runs

entries.name: Version Datasets and Adapters Together as One Artifact Pair
Version System Prompts With the Same Discipline as Application Code
Version Eval Datasets the Same Way You Version Code
vLLM
entries.description: High-throughput inference and serving engine for LLMs with batching and OpenAI-compatible APIs

entries.name: Voyage AI
entries.description: Embedding and reranking models that consistently top retrieval benchmarks, now part of MongoDB

entries.name: Weights & Biases Weave
entries.description: An observability and evaluation toolkit for AI applications from Weights & Biases

entries.name: A Survey on Large Language Model based Autonomous Agents
entries.description: The reference taxonomy for LLM agents: a unified four-module framework — profile, memory, planning, action — that organizes agent construction, applications, and evaluation; the profiling/memory/planning/action vocabulary most agent frameworks now use

entries.name: Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models
entries.description: First LLM-powered lifelong-learning agent in Minecraft: an automatic curriculum, an ever-growing skill library of verified executable code, and iterative environment-feedback prompting — the origin of the 'skill library' pattern now reappearing in agent skill systems

entries.name: Weaviate
entries.description: Open-source vector database combining object storage, vector search, filtering, and hybrid retrieval

entries.name: Web Scraping Tools
entries.description: Curated tools for web scraping workflows in AI engineering

entries.name: Weekly ArXiv Picks
entries.description: Template for weekly AI engineering paper picks and maintainer review

entries.name: Chain-of-Thought Prompting Elicits Reasoning in Large Language Models
entries.description: Showed prompting a large enough model to produce intermediate reasoning steps improves multi-step reasoning accuracy -- use CoT for reasoning-heavy tasks, but don't trust the trace as an accurate explanation of the model's actual computation

entries.name: Jailbroken: How Does LLM Safety Training Fail?
entries.description: Explained why jailbreaks work via two failure modes — competing objectives (helpfulness vs safety) and mismatched generalization (safety training doesn't cover what pretraining can do) — and showed scale alone won't fix them; the conceptual framework behind LLM red-teaming

entries.name: Weights & Biases
entries.description: Experiment tracking and model management platform for ML and AI teams

entries.name: Whisper
entries.description: OpenAI's open-source speech recognition model: robust multilingual transcription and translation trained on 680k hours of audio

entries.name: Windsurf
entries.description: Agentic AI code editor built around Cascade, a context-aware agent that keeps working across your whole repo

entries.name: Qwen2.5-Math Technical Report: Toward Mathematical Expert Model via Self-Improvement
entries.description: Documented a self-improvement pipeline (using the model to generate and filter its own better math training data) producing strong math-specific models -- narrow applicability, and Qwen's own successors have since surpassed these results

entries.name: SWE-agent: Agent-Computer Interfaces Enable Automated Software Engineering
entries.description: Showed a custom agent-computer interface (simplified LM-friendly commands for editing/testing code) matters as much as model quality -- treat interface design, not just model choice, as a first-class variable for coding agents

entries.name: Yann LeCun
entries.description: Longtime AI researcher with influential perspective on representation learning and AI systems

entries.name: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models
entries.description: Showed interleaving reasoning traces with external actions and their observations outperforms reasoning-only or acting-only prompting, meaning the reason-act-observe loop should be your default agent architecture pattern before reaching for something more complex

entries.name: Tree of Thoughts: Deliberate Problem Solving with Large Language Models
entries.description: Showed exploring and backtracking across multiple candidate reasoning paths beats single-chain CoT on hard search-style problems -- reach for ToT for problems needing exploration/backtracking, not as a default CoT replacement

entries.name: Yi
entries.description: 01.AI open model family with bilingual and long-context variants from small to mid-large sizes

entries.name: Zep
entries.description: Memory and context engineering platform for AI agents and assistants

entries.name: Judging LLM-as-a-Judge with MT-Bench and Chatbot Arena
entries.description: Showed strong LLM judges agree with human judgments over 80% of the time, matching human-human agreement -- use LLM-as-judge for scalable open-ended evaluation, but mitigate its documented position, verbosity, and self-enhancement biases

entries.name: COLLEAGUE.SKILL: Automated AI Skill Generation via Expert Knowledge Distillation
entries.description: Automates trace-to-skill distillation: turns a person's heterogeneous work traces into a versioned, inspectable agent skill package with separate capability and behavior tracks -- treat expert knowledge capture as a pipeline, not manual skill authoring

entries.name: zvec
entries.description: Lightweight, in-process vector database from Alibaba for local RAG and agent memory
